_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The ICAP Status Report 2026 is an analytical and knowledge-exchange publication, not itself a registration instrument. It documents the registration and reporting obligations imposed by each ETS on regulated entities. For the workspace as a freight forwarder, the relevant registration obligations arise from the underlying ETS regulations in each jurisdiction, not from ICAP directly.

**UK ETS:** Regulated entities (aircraft operators and, from 1 July 2026, domestic maritime ship operators) must register with the UK ETS registry and submit annual Emissions Monitoring Plans (EMPs). Ship operators entering the UK ETS maritime scope from 1 July 2026 must prepare EMPs, appoint accredited verifiers, and file annual verified emissions reports under an operator-based MRV framework. The workspace is not a regulated entity under current scope but must collect carrier-level registration status and EMP documentation as part of supplier onboarding.

**EU ETS:** Aircraft operators and shipping companies are registered entities. The EU MRV Regulation governs maritime monitoring, reporting, and verification. From 2026, shipping companies must account for 100% of covered emissions (CO₂, and now CH4 and N2O). The workspace should collect evidence of carrier EU ETS registration and verified emissions reports as part of its Scope 3 emissions data-collection programme.

**Japan GX-ETS:** The mandatory system requires companies with annual CO₂ emissions ≥100,000 tonnes to participate. FY2026 (April 2026 onward) is the first mandatory calculation period; full trading is expected to become operational in Autumn 2027. Companies apply for allowance allocation based on rules disclosed by end of 2025. Offsets are limited to J-Credits (domestic) and JCM Credits (Article 6.2). The workspace should collect GX-ETS registration status from Japan-based airline and road-freight carrier partners and track whether logistics firms in the workspace's supply chain meet the 100,000-tonne threshold.

**Data fields the workspace must collect from carriers in all covered jurisdictions:** ETS registry ID or operator permit number; annual verified emissions (tCO₂e) for covered routes or activities; allowance procurement strategy; carbon surcharge methodology and per-unit rate; verification body identity; reporting period covered. These fields support both commercial cost-transparency and the workspace's own Scope 3 Category 4 (upstream transportation) disclosures.

**Published vs. promised:** EU CBAM definitive-stage rules are published and in force for 2026 imports. Japan GX-ETS aviation and shipping benchmark formulas are promised but not yet released (MLIT process ongoing as of the report's February 2026 editorial cut-off). UK ETS second free-allocation period rules for 2027–2030 are confirmed in the November 2025 Free Allocation Review response but the CBAM phase-out trajectory is set at indicative levels pending final legislation.

*Source: ICAP Status Report 2026: Emissions Trading Worldwide, ICAP, 14 April 2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/publications/emissions-trading-worldwide-icap-status-report-2026. UK ETS Factsheet, ICAP ETS Map, ICAP. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets/uk-emissions-trading-scheme-uk-ets. Japan GX-ETS Factsheet, ICAP ETS Map, ICAP. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets/japan-gx-ets. Greenhouse Gas Emissions Trading Scheme (Amendment) (Extension to Maritime Activities) Order 2026, UK Parliament/House of Lords Library, 2026. https://lordslibrary.parliament.uk/greenhouse-gas-emissions-trading-scheme-amendment-extension-to-maritime-activities-order-2026/*
